stem: A jar contains 50 coins consisting of only nickels and quarters. The total value of the coins is $8.50. How many quarters are in the jar?
explanation: Let q = number of quarters and n = number of nickels. From the problem: n + q = 50 (total coins) and 5n + 25q = 850 (total value in cents). From the first equation: n = 50 - q. Substituting into the second equation: 5(50 - q) + 25q = 850. Simplifying: 250 - 5q + 25q = 850, so 20q + 250 = 850. Subtracting 250 from both sides: 20q = 600. Dividing by 20: q = 30.

distractorRationale.A: Found the number of nickels instead of quarters: n = 50 - 30 = 20
distractorRationale.B: Assumed equal numbers of each coin type: 50 ÷ 2 = 25
distractorRationale.C: Correct: Set up system n + q = 50 and 5n + 25q = 850, substitute n = 50 - q, solve 20q = 600 to get q = 30
distractorRationale.D: Divided total value in cents by the value of a quarter: 850 ÷ 25 = 34
